threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still don’t know the different bus routes that can get you to Dalston without having to transfer twice. Everyone else does.
Caught a bloke singing opera on the 133 last night, completely out of tune but the whole bus clapped. Brixton at its best, no shame in the madness.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I saw a neighbor feeding strays from her window while her kids played in the street—this place feels like a big quirky family.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do you find spontaneous parties in abandoned warehouses, where everyone just shows up with their own drinks and gets lost in the moment.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can tell a local by how they instinctively know to avoid BART during rush hour after a breakdown. It’s like a survival skill here.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 14 tonight packed with folks wearing mismatched shoes. It was like a fashion show of lost items after the BART mess. Only in SF.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think Gràcia is just for partying; they miss the midday hush when the locals retreat inside for their siestas. It's a different vibe altogether.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by how they struggle to find the right 'café con leche' spot instead of just hitting the tourist traps on every corner.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch every time a bus zooms past—vets say it takes three months to join the Dalston dodge.
Caught a night bus from Brixton and overheard two blokes debating whether a chicken shop ever beats a kebab stand. This is the real South London dilemma.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: The moment I spotted an impromptu dance party in the U-Bahn while waiting for yet another delayed train on a Monday morning.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin do buses have their own breaks for strikes. You’ll see them parked, but the tram just keeps rolling, like nothing's wrong.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they instinctively cross 16th St. when they hear the BART announcement, dodging the hustle like it’s second nature.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a guy in a tuxedo giving his date a tour of the bus stops like they were art installations. Only in SF can transit be a romantic backdrop.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists love the parks, but they miss the real vibe in the plaça during evening closures—locals gather, swap stories, and share meals.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell by the heavy backpacks filled with guidebooks; locals know the best spots aren't in the brochures.
